content:
# Rayon CPU Parallelization — Decision Record (2026-04-02)

## What
Added rayon parallelism to 4 CPU-bound operations in dorea-lut and dorea-hsl (issue #10).
Merged to main via feat/10-rayon-cpu-parallelization.

## Key Design Decisions

1. **rayon = "1" at workspace level, not per-crate** — single version pin, only wired into dorea-lut and dorea-hsl. dorea-video and dorea-cli explicitly excluded.

2. **par_iter_mut().zip().zip().for_each() for apply_depth_luts** — flat zip chain (not nested) is idiomatic rayon for 3-way parallel iteration. luts is &DepthLuts shared read-only; DepthLuts contains only Vec<f32>/Vec<LutGrid> which are Sync.

3. **par_chunks_mut(width).enumerate() for Sobel + Gaussian blur** — row granularity is correct for image convolutions. H-pass and V-pass must remain SEQUENTIAL (not rayon::join) because the V-pass reads temp which the H-pass writes. The for_each() call blocks until complete, so sequencing is safe.

4. **find_nearest extracted as pure function + par_iter().map().collect()** — parallel gather over empty cells, then sequential write-back. Empty cells are guaranteed distinct (built from triple-nested loop over unique (ri,gi,bi) indices). type CellIdx = [usize; 3] added for clarity.

5. **QualifierAccum struct for fold/reduce** — 5-tuple (f64,f64,f64,f64,f64) was rejected as unreadable magic indices. Named struct with zero()/combine() methods is idiomatic and zero-cost. fold() and reduce() use same identity (QualifierAccum::zero).

6. **Outer qualifier loop stays serial** — 6-8 qualifiers × par_iter on full pixel array. Parallelizing the outer loop would over-subscribe the rayon thread pool and cause contention. Serial outer + parallel inner is the right granularity.

7. **f64 accumulation in fold/reduce** — floating-point reduction order differs ~1e-6 from serial path. Acceptable within test tolerances.

## Review Findings Worth Remembering

- IDE/rust-analyzer reports `use rayon::prelude::*` as unused import — false positive. cargo clippy correctly sees the traits as used. Don't remove the import based on IDE warnings.
- needless_range_loop fires when `row[x]` is assigned inside `for x in 0..row.len()` even if x is also used to compute other indices. Fix: `for (x, out) in row.iter_mut().enumerate()` with `*out = value`.
- par_chunks_mut(width) always produces chunks of exactly width (last chunk may be shorter if n % width != 0, but n = width*height so n is always divisible by width for well-formed images). Added assert_eq!(n, width * height) to document and enforce this invariant.

## What Blocked / Surprised

- Local `main` branch was behind origin/main by 2 commits at branch creation; ran git pull origin main --rebase to update before starting work.
- IDE diagnostics (rust-analyzer) consistently reported false-positive unused-import warnings for rayon::prelude in all 3 modified files. Every time: cargo clippy -p dorea-lut -p dorea-hsl -- -D warnings passed clean.
- Pre-existing clippy warnings in dorea-gpu/src/cpu.rs (unused mean_d variable, useless u8 comparison) showed up in workspace-wide cargo clippy but are not our crates to fix.
